programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The crew of Warwickshire, Northamptonshire, Derbyshire, Leicestershire and Rutland Air Ambulance race to reach Penny, who is in anaphylactic shock after a wasp sting.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 11-year-old is brought in after being struck by a car. A woman has tripped over her cat. And a doctor describes the painful decision to live away from home for the pandemic.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Jon and Kay want to expand their tearoom business by converting a horse box into a mobile coffee shop. And Carina researches doggy day care for her caravan site.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Sidhbh and Neasa prepare their brand-new Miami clinic for its first clients. Laura and Lindsay steel themselves for the eye-watering cost of childbirth in Florida.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, the Garners squeeze Kay and Nick's budget to find them the perfect place. In Murcia, the Browns help busy brothers Alex and Cyrille find a dream holiday pad.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton is house-hunting around the beautiful towns and villages of the Costa Tropical in Spain for Susan and her partner Ilse. They're looking for a house with countryside views.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Allie and Cliff have decided to leave the UK and find their new forever home on the beautiful Greek island of Crete. Ben Hillman has been challenged to find the best properties around for their £240,000 budget.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Debbie and Graham want to find their very own holiday home in the beautiful town of Oliva, in the south of Spain's Valencia region. Sara Damergi has conducted a rigorous search and come up with five great options for their £80,000 budget.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: In Brittany, Paul hefts steel girders and cuts through reinforced concrete 16 feet off the ground. And a wedding party are trapped by dangerous trees near Cognac.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kris and Emily wanted to add a fourth bedroom to their crammed three-bed home in Somersham, but Kirstie was convinced they already had enough space, and embarked on her biggest-ever declutter.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Swansea, performing arts director Anthony plans a theatre-themed menu for his guests, Ross, Tula, Kola and Julie. But straight-talking Tula tries to steal the show with a candid revelation.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Swansea, salesman Ross plans a night of culinary sophistication to wow his guests, who he thinks lack class. But will his plan to cook everything to his taste, including a very rare beef Wellington, backfire?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Social carer Kola plans to bring some high-class hip-hop swagger to the contest in Swansea. He rolls out a red carpet welcome for his guests and hopes that his Nigerian menu will educate and impress them.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Triathlete Tula brings a taste of the Costa Brava to Swansea, with a Spanish-themed menu and dress code. She hopes that a combination of charm and chorizo can help her avoid a punch up over the paella.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Social worker Julie plans a chilled out slumber party for the final night of the week in Swansea. Julie cooks a homely menu, but struggles with the pronunciation as well as the preparation of some of her ingredients, which doesn't surprise Anthony.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Hotels
programme.desc: Exploring unique and surprising hotels throughout the world with innovative features and spectacular settings, from cabins dangling from clifftops to clandestine subterranean crash pads.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Emily and Lucy are spoilt for choice when Kirstie gives them multiple options near the Leeds hospital where they work. And Sarah and Tom hope Phil can find the perfect balance.

programme.title: The Equalizer
programme.desc: A former commando who had faked his own death comes out of retirement to rescue a young girl from violent Russian mobsters.

programme.title: Moon
programme.desc: As his three-year lone mission nears its end, astronaut Sam Bell begins to suffer surreal experiences which lead him to believe things aboard the base are not what they seem.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Joe and Phil Wang take in Washington DC's wonders, as they tick off the world's biggest obelisk, Barbie Pond and a 'secret door experience', before finishing with a signature Gin Rickey.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley host the battle of numbers, letters and laughs. Roisin Conaty and Phil Wang play Joe Wilkinson and Lou Sanders. With David O'Doherty.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley host the battle of numbers, letters and laughs. Roisin Conaty and Phil Wang play Joe Wilkinson and Lou Sanders. With David O'Doherty.

programme.title: Wonders of the World I Can't See
programme.desc: Chris travels to the ancient city of Petra in Jordan with Guz Khan, where floating, haggling, camel riding and some unique descriptions from Guz await.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: When a fully loaded HGV rolls over on the A6, Fluff and his team need to create a second mess to solve the first. Jim is sent to recover a broken down truck from a waste tip facility, but working among the rubbish is not particularly pleasant.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The team attempt to intercept a drug-dealer travelling into the county.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A collection of some of the most extreme chases the Interceptors have been involved in, including a hot pursuit through a housing estate and a face-off with a 20-ton farm truck.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Interceptor Kev reveals how to spot a drunk driver, before he gets behind a bolshy bandit with no regard for the speed limit, while Jacko follows an all-terrain tearaway.

programme.title: Day of Anger
programme.desc: A poor street cleaner is relentlessly bullied by the people of a small western township, but things change when a ruthless gunfighter rides into the town.

programme.title: The Redhead and the Cowboy
programme.desc: A government spy joins a pair of Southern sympathizers planning to smuggle a message to Confederate irregulars.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Marcus Charman and colleague Trigger patch up an HGV's fuel tank during evening rush hour, Tez resorts to using a blowtorch to fix a car transporter's snapped spring, and a broken down driver gives out the wrong location.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Operation Fuego's first hit involves John and South Yorkshire officers givingchase to a getaway driver in a stolen vehicle taken in a burglary, which is now on a 120mph rampagealong the M18 near Doncaster.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team pulls over a very angry driver, a car thief leads the officers on a cross-county chase, and the Air Support Unit has its work cut out pursuing an athletic burglar.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drunk driver leads the interceptors on a merry-go-round. The team joins forces with the chopper to track down a wanted criminal on a motorbike, and a young driver crashes straight into an unmarked police car.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: A car has broken down on the hard shoulder of the M6. PC Andy Doran is called in to support Highways, as they're unhappy with the driver's behaviour. When he arrives at the location, Andy quickly finds out why.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Lisa Kelly finds herself stranded hundreds of miles from the nearest town. Darrell Ward takes an off-road shortcut, and Art Burke encounters a melting ice crossing and a full load.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: A computer hacker's access to secret government files may threaten the lives of agents Mulder and Scully.

programme.title: Scam City
programme.desc: As tourists pour in and day turns to night, Prague's scammers emerge disguised as beautiful girls, harmless hawkers and friendly taxi drivers, all aiming to swindle unsuspecting tourists out of their cash.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Extreme weather and crumbling infrastructure create tough conditions for Mark in Micronesia. And Sludge travels deep into the outback for cattle stations in need of diesel fuel.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Kev pepper-sprays a suspect resisting arrest, while Richie and his colleagues TPAC a stolen car in the snow, Spike raids a cannabis farm, and Paul rushes to rescue two people whose car is crushed by a falling tree.

programme.title: The Alamo
programme.desc: Star-studded saga of the 1836 defence of the Alamo, when a 187-strong garrison including William Travis and Jim Bowie fought to save Texas from Santa Anna's Mexican army for 13 days. The rivalry of the two leaders was tempered by the arrival of Davy Crockett, who brought his Tennessee volunteers to fight alongside them.

programme.title: The Alamo
programme.desc: Star-studded saga of the 1836 defence of the Alamo, when a 187-strong garrison including William Travis and Jim Bowie fought to save Texas from Santa Anna's Mexican army for 13 days. The rivalry of the two leaders was tempered by the arrival of Davy Crockett, who brought his Tennessee volunteers to fight alongside them.

programme.title: Two Mules for Sister Sara
programme.desc: Whimsical but engaging western about a drifter who saves a woman from a gang of marauders. The cowboy is astonished to learn that the woman is a nun, especially when he witnesses her heavy drinking and cigar-smoking ways. The unlikely pair embark on a voyage of discovery as they become embroiled in the Mexican War.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: On one of the UK's busiest roads, a jackknifed HGV has careered through a front garden, over a ditch and blocked a lane of traffic. Meanwhile, Jim's mettle is tested and Steve has to quell a rebellion.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Ant and Mark's dogged pursuit of a stolen moped takes them off their patch into the estates of Sheffield.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Ange Salous confronts a not-so-happy chappy, and a torrential downpour hampers police at a major crash scene on the M1.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors embark on a spooky pursuit as their search for an armed drink-driver leads them through a graveyard. Elsewhere, a romantic night on the town goes awry when the police break up a boozy brawl, and a man is given a curb-side lesson in road safety after attempting to use a moped as a minibus.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PCs Chris Swash and Rowan Burt are chasing a vehicle through the roads of Chester that is refusing to stop. Plus, PC Ian Cosgrove is on the scene of a serious crash. Fortunately, there are no serious injuries.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, a police dog unit is called in to track down a pair who have gone missing after a late-night crash. Meanwhile, two drunks are restrained by officers for suspected assault.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A cyclist gives deputies the runaround; a boyfriend saves his girlfriend from jail on her birthday.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A bachelorette party is rescued from a sinking boat. A group of spring breakers is forced to pour out liquor. A man is caught selling beer from a backpack. A boiled peanut vendor drifts toward danger.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: The FBI continues its search for the computer disk believed to contain information about aliens.

programme.title: Scam City
programme.desc: As millions of tourists descend on the city for the Carnival, it's party time for Rio's scammers as well.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: In the final stages of transporting cargo to Micronesia, Mark faces volatile weather and unstable roads. Brad contracts to move a road building camp deep in the outback.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nation's roads.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: This week, disaster strikes on a small country lane in Leicestershire when a 40-tonne truck jackknifes across both lanes. The road is blocked, causing traffic chaos and Crouch recovery operative Stig jumps in one of their military machines.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler has a brief fling with Rachel's boss Joanna which leaves him handcuffed without his trousers in her office. Meanwhile, Joey can only afford one book in an encyclopaedia set, and Monica caters a party for her mother.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ballroom Dancing
programme.desc: Joey argues with the building superintendent Treegar and ends up having to help him practise ballroom dancing. Meanwhile, Ross and Chandler can't get out of their gym membership even though neither of them wants to exercise, and Phoebe loses her massage job for kissing a client.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: In Fuengirola, bar owners Andy and Matt put on a Bollywood themed evening. They've hired a dancer to perform, Andy's busy in the kitchen and Matt's looking after publicity.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Benidorm-based entertainer Dean performs as Liam Gallagher in an Oasis tribute. But he has a new song to learn and a new tambourine to source before the show.

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Chris takes on some elusive mice causing havoc in an unexpected location. In Buckinghamshire, a lorry load of potentially hazardous waste has been dumped by the side of a main road.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A Ford Fiesta stolen by teenagers leads the cops on a chase through Bradford. A gang of burglars who have been targeting pensioners are spotted in Halifax. The road crime team are tracking a team of burglars who operate in a convoy of cars.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: At Christmas the traffic cops have their hands full as party-going drunks end up getting behind the wheel. The stresses of surviving Yuletide lead to late night argy-bargy and screaming matches.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Interceptors join with the National Police Air Service to stop two suspects in a car believed to be on cloned plates. When units on the ground strike with a stinger, the driver swerves off the road and takes the pursuit into some corn fields.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Firearms officers enter a property to discover an arsenal of weapons and a recently harvested cannabis crop, and suspicions are aroused by a lad in a balaclava on a sunny day.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Following the work of the Firearms Squad. The team respond to reports of a shotgun changing hands in the suburbs. When the team swoops in, both buyer and seller are taken by surprise.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: A dubious pothole injury threatens to put a local council out of pocket when a £65,000 claim comes in, and a farmer forgets the bus he alleges hit his van is covered in cameras. (S2 Ep9)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Brax, Ricky and Casey throw a football in the sand, loving the beach and their new sense of freedom.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Aunties-to-be Eden and Abigail are excited, handing Levi a baby Fowler outfit, but his downcast mood causes them to form a plan.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Wyne has some advice for Elvira, a Romanian woman, who has brought a cocktail of drugs from her home country to 'treat' her many conditions.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Sister Rachel is in charge of the hub today and the pressure is already mounting. A diabetic's sugar levels put him at risk of going into a coma, a young boy struggles to stay awake after a head injury, and a woman is brought in by her hairdresser.

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Lifting the lid on the night shift as experienced by A&E workers, with exclusive access to Hull Royal Infirmary. The night-time challenges facing NHS staff are captured in their raw form.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Following specialist emergency response teams on their most extreme medical call outs, embedded with the Critical Care and Air Ambulance Response cars.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.title: Babies 24/7: The Maternity Ward
programme.desc: The reality of childbirth is laid bare when following expectant parents hoping for the perfect birth at The Royal Preston Hospital's award-winning Maternity Unit, where nearly 4500 babies are born every year.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The staff face a flurry of bowel problems, including coeliac disease, cancer and a complete pelvic repair.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Four-year-old Skyler is rushed into Paediatrics care with recurring seizures; 65-year-old Shaun has tumbled down some steps; and Eric, 85, has a badly swollen leg.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Professional bass player Caleb has a lump on his forehead that he wants Dr Dev to remove as it's giving him a headache. It's gunk galore as Dr Emma operates on a chest cyst, while Dr Adil debates whether he can remove a large keloid.

programme.title: Happy Birthday to You
programme.desc: Make your child a star on BabyTV by creating their very own personalized birthday greeting. Using a digital photo, parents can create a clip for their child which can be viewed online or submitted to BabyTV for the chance of being featured on-air.

programme.title: Bath Tubbies
programme.desc: Ducky, Ellie and Froggy are asleep on a boat when a sudden wave soaks them. They see the splashing dolphin in the water and enjoy the dolphin's amusing tricks.

programme.title: Draco
programme.desc: Join Draco as he learns the difference between opposites - under and on. As he tries to figure out how to play with a jumping ball, he has lots of fun understanding what it means to sit under a jumping ball and to sit and bounce on one!

programme.title: The World Is A Wonder
programme.desc: Come and explore the peach tree as it changes by season! In the summer sun, the peaches are ripe and heavy! In the autumn, the leaves change colours! In the cold winter, the tree is coated with snow! In the spring.

programme.title: Zeina's Detective Agency
programme.desc: A highly familiar day-to-day object arrives at Zeina's doorstep forgetting what it is used for. Searching for the answer, Zeina and her best friend Roy come up with a new and hilarious use for the surprised object.

programme.title: Welcome to the Island
programme.desc: Coco, the wise coconut tree, Bucky, the curious rock, Lily, the little flower, and Blossom, the happy papaya tree, explore themes of fostering and building friendships while having fun on their small island.

programme.title: Teeny and Tiny's Classroom
programme.desc: Teeny and Tiny are two happy and mischievous friends that share with us their riddles and games. As we follow the antics of the tiny friends, we discover shapes, colours and quantities.

programme.title: Mike's Bike
programme.sub-title: Johnny and Tommy are Super heroes

programme.title: Stick With Mick
programme.desc: Mick is on his way to the swimming pool. He places water wings on his arms and dives into the pool.

programme.title: Crafty Rafty

programme.title: Jungle Book

programme.title: Songs & Rhymes
programme.desc: Charlie has fun in the Bath! Join him as he washes his hair, scrubs himself with soap, makes bubbles and plays with his rubber duck. When he's nice and clean he puts on his pyjamas and goes to bed. Good night Charlie!

programme.title: The Lollipops Song Series
programme.desc: Three toddler friends who form a girl band and turn everyday ups and downs into fun.

programme.title: Hungry Henry
programme.desc: Henry wants to eat chips but Chef George is out of potatoes. Join Hungry Henry on his journey to find potatoes. There aren't any at the grocery store or at the market, but there are potatoes in the field, where they grow! Come along and enjoy amazing chips with Henry!

programme.title: Mice Builders
programme.desc: Mice are devoted to building their new house.

programme.title: Pix & Leo
programme.desc: Leo and Pix are looking for the perfect place to have a picnic. While Leo takes a nap, Pix finds a new friend to play with.

programme.title: My Robot & I

programme.title: Walter & Dude

programme.title: Mowgli - Welcome to the Jungle

programme.title: Personal Cinema: Maggie Smith

programme.title: Tea With Mussolini
programme.desc: A loose-knit group of gentile lady ex-pats adopt illegitimate youngster Luca, and haunt the art world and cafes of Florence, oblivious to the rise of Mussolini's fascists. When the war starts to impinge on their lives, they find themselves interned, awaiting rescue from the invading British and American armies.

programme.title: How to Get Ahead

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Bank Devalued Our House After Approving Our Mortgage
programme.desc: The team investigate why a bank suddenly refused to let one couple re-mortgage because of the way their home had been constructed - despite approving the mortgage five years earlier.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion struggles to find fault with a mid-terrace in Stoke. Plus, another terrace in Staffordshire requires more attention, and a flat in South East London has Jacqui fretting over the short lease.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion heads to an antiques fair in the town of Llandeilo in West Wales, where experts Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson help the red and blue teams as they compete to see who can buy the three most profitable items to take to auction. Christina also gets hands-on with the ancient art of silversmithing at a family business in the heart of nearby town Laugharne.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Rav and Nick listen in to calls by a scammer posing as Sky's broadband department as he tries to steal money from UK victims believing he is fixing their internet connection.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Television and radio presenter Angellica Bell, and husband, Michael Underwood, call upon the Escape expertise of Nicki Chapman to find them a family home in rural Devon.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having been to the Lake District over 50 times, today's couple have decided to stop visiting and start living in one of the UK's most spectacular National Parks. The about-to-be-retired couple need Steve Brown's help to find them, and their two rescue cats, a new forever home in the Cumbrian countryside.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.sub-title: Glasgow - A Fix at the Top of the Cathedral
programme.desc: Dom and Lucia help an iconic Glasgow landmark that has lost its lustre.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four - in this episode, friends Kevin, Dave, Alan and Ken - take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: Eurovision Week continues with the second semi-final, live from Vienna, as 15 new acts vie for just ten places in Saturday's grand final. UK viewers can vote for their favourites, and UK act Look Mum No Computer takes to the Eurovision stage for his first live performance of Eins, Zwei, Drei. As a member of the Big Four, the UK is guaranteed a place in Saturday's final, along with Eurovision champions and hosts Austria.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former care centre in Waterlooville is converted into a smart family home for a young couple, their baby daughter, ten pet dogs and their dog grooming business. programme.sub-title: Midlands - A Celestial Projector is Brought Back to Life
programme.desc: The team help a charity get their iconic bus back on the road, Mark Stuckey reaches for the stars fixing an unusual projector, and Will is wowed by the lost art of encaustic tiles.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp has his hands full as he presents this quiz, where a team of celebrity drag queens, featuring The Vivienne, Tia Kofi, Kitty Scott-Claus, and Lawrence Chaney, get together to take on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charity. One by one, they face the bridge, which has 32 potential steps - 22 truths and 10 lies. They must only step on the truths, avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer.

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Bank Devalued Our House After Approving Our Mortgage
programme.desc: The team investigate why a bank suddenly refused to let one couple re-mortgage because of the way their home had been constructed - despite approving the mortgage five years earlier.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion struggles to find fault with a mid-terrace in Stoke. Plus, another terrace in Staffordshire requires more attention, and a flat in South East London has Jacqui fretting over the short lease.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion heads to an antiques fair in the town of Llandeilo in West Wales, where experts Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson help the red and blue teams as they compete to see who can buy the three most profitable items to take to auction. Christina also gets hands-on with the ancient art of silversmithing at a family business in the heart of nearby town Laugharne.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Rav and Nick listen in to calls by a scammer posing as Sky's broadband department as he tries to steal money from UK victims believing he is fixing their internet connection.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Television and radio presenter Angellica Bell, and husband, Michael Underwood, call upon the Escape expertise of Nicki Chapman to find them a family home in rural Devon.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having been to the Lake District over 50 times, today's couple have decided to stop visiting and start living in one of the UK's most spectacular National Parks. The about-to-be-retired couple need Steve Brown's help to find them, and their two rescue cats, a new forever home in the Cumbrian countryside.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.sub-title: Glasgow - A Fix at the Top of the Cathedral
programme.desc: Dom and Lucia help an iconic Glasgow landmark that has lost its lustre.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four - in this episode, friends Kevin, Dave, Alan and Ken - take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.desc: Featuring most talked-about stories in the UK and interactions with guests on the green sofa.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Follow the latest dramas of the residents of Albert Square in London's East End as they deal with life, love and loss.

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: Eurovision Week continues with the second semi-final, live from Vienna, as 15 new acts vie for just ten places in Saturday's grand final. UK viewers can vote for their favourites, and UK act Look Mum No Computer takes to the Eurovision stage for his first live performance of Eins, Zwei, Drei. As a member of the Big Four, the UK is guaranteed a place in Saturday's final, along with Eurovision champions and hosts Austria.

One by one, they face the bridge, which has 32 potential steps - 22 truths and 10 lies. They must only step on the truths, avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Bank Devalued Our House After Approving Our Mortgage
programme.desc: The team investigate why a bank suddenly refused to let one couple re-mortgage because of the way their home had been constructed - despite approving the mortgage five years earlier.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion struggles to find fault with a mid-terrace in Stoke. Plus, another terrace in Staffordshire requires more attention, and a flat in South East London has Jacqui fretting over the short lease.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion heads to an antiques fair in the town of Llandeilo in West Wales, where experts Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson help the red and blue teams as they compete to see who can buy the three most profitable items to take to auction. Christina also gets hands-on with the ancient art of silversmithing at a family business in the heart of nearby town Laugharne.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having been to the Lake District over 50 times, today's couple have decided to stop visiting and start living in one of the UK's most spectacular National Parks. The about-to-be-retired couple need Steve Brown's help to find them, and their two rescue cats, a new forever home in the Cumbrian countryside.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four - in this episode, friends Kevin, Dave, Alan and Ken - take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money.

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: Eurovision Week continues with the second semi-final, live from Vienna, as 15 new acts vie for just ten places in Saturday's grand final. As a member of the Big Four, the UK is guaranteed a place in Saturday's final, along with Eurovision champions and hosts Austria.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.title: Despicable Me 3
programme.desc: After being fired from the `Anti-Villain League', Gru travels to Freedonia to meet his long-lost twin brother. Once united the siblings find themselves on a mission to take down a former child star from the 1980s, now out for revenge.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team find themselves split between Saint Marie and the island of Saint Auguste as they attempt to investigate the mysterious death of a British tourist. On the surface, it looks like a clear-cut accident, but nothing is quite what it seems.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Our queens get Stewart ready for his first ever date and invite themselves along as well.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie
programme.desc: London publicist Edina Monsoon and best friend Patsy Stone flee to the French Riviera after accidentally knocking super model Kate Moss into the River Thames.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Fresh new talent aim to impress famous faces and follow in the footsteps of some of stand-up's biggest names. Across a number of rounds, comedians compete to show they could be the next breakout comedy star.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: This is an average story of a very average woman. Of Spud, carer for her father, who cooks, cleans and serves badly and half-heartedly. A woman who is never seen or thanked. Ignored. A woman who, in spite of what society tells her, does not feel bad about herself. Who, despite not being young, or a mother, or, worst of all, beautiful, is actually quite happy in herself, if not her life. Then, one day, her dad dies, making her realise that she may have to come out of the shadows and be seen.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps mother and daughter, Diana and Millie, seek out space to bloom in the Somerset countryside and also meets a couple who have planted their very own vineyard.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure in Uzbekistan continues in Samarkand, one of the oldest cities in Central Asia.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Set against the dramatic backdrop of the Malvern Hills, Gardeners' World comes from the spectacular RHS Malvern Spring Festival in Worcestershire.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the fourth heat of this series of the classic quiz. The specialist subjects are detectorists, the 'Alice' books of Lewis Carroll, British politics in the 1960s and Sir Elton John.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team visit Chepstow to help a multigenerational family of four create a beautiful home. They have to clean up after a menagerie of pets, create storage solutions and modernise the family dining room.

programme.title: Super Smart Animals
programme.desc: From skateboarding dogs to chimp math geniuses, Liz Bonnin embarks on a worldwide search for the planet's most intelligent animals. Liz gets creative with dolphins, shares a eureka moment with orangutans and defends the reputation of the human race when she goes head to head with a chimp genius in a test of maths and memory.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: In the gardens of Knightshayes Court in Devon, the team of experts uncover a copy of a play with royalty on the cast list and a suite of jewellery which was probably worn at the court of Napoleon. Plus, there are some fabulous Doulton vases and an incredibly rare 17th-century jewellery box.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael uncovers the Central Asian origins of the apple in the lush oasis surrounding Samarkand, and stands in the shadow of the fortified Ark of Bukhara.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Emma's spy cameras reveal a host of cute surprises.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: A brand-new batch of amateur cooks enter the MasterChef kitchen, ready to compete in the first round and take their shot at culinary stardom.

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The RNLI respond to over 9,000 shouts each year - and what often gets people into trouble in the first place are the hidden dangers. In Porthcawl, on the south west coast of Wales, the crew receive a call about a swimmer who's been swept out to sea in a rip current - as his wife and son watch on helplessly from the beach.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.sub-title: Blakeney Point - Bass and Sea Trout
programme.desc: Paul, Bob and trusty terrier Ted travel to Blakeney Point in North Norfolk for a sea fishing adventure. Blakeney Point is a four-mile spit of shingle and sand beach bank, with the North Sea on one side and the Glaven Estuary on the other.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons face a difficult dilemma in the middle of a life-saving bile duct cancer operation. A chest sarcoma proves more challenging than expected, with no room for error.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Meticulous and methodical, Big John engineers his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it's flawless. But as the deadline approaches, nothing goes according to plan, and two battles unfold simultaneously: a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable. Precision planning meets a chaotic reality, and one miscalculation on either side could mean catastrophe.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It's time for the second heat of the week, with six more home cooks putting themselves in the culinary spotlight, ready to show the judges that they have what it takes.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The eight remaining designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London. They tackle an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charity's ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers before Michelle and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces.

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty flails as Ning continues to push him. Meanwhile, Leanne and Gary bond, until he pressures Leanne to betray Matty to safeguard her family.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Stamford in Lincolnshire, where Michael Hogben is left stunned when one seller calls him stingy and another fulfils her nan's last wishes by coming on the show. Will Henry splash the cash? Joining the team today are Henry Nicholls and Jan Keyne.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Rene and Casey take on rival couple Jarrod and Brandi, Darrell wins an intriguing locker after dodging a bullet and Ivy encounters a furry friend.

programme.desc: The bidders return to Dallas hoping to land a locker of a lifetime. Mary flouts the law in her eagerness to score a dream unit.

programme.desc: Discover the link between Nazis in New Mexico and a mystery craft in Roswell, explore Australia's underground labyrinthine abyss and decode the cypher of Atomic Island. (S5, ep 19)

programme.desc: An exploration of crime and punishment in ancient Egypt filled with homicidal royalty, rampant tomb raiding and acts of extreme punishment.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy tries to continue his winning streak, Rene and Darrell play mind games and Jarrod and Brandi visit a crime scene and travel back in time.

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.desc: Ursula declares war on Roy at an auction in the Old Weston neighbourhood of Toronto.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Paul and Bogart put away all their gimmicks and tricks for an auction in Leaside.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers are at Bolton Wanderers FC. Memorabilia from the legendary Hacienda club turns up for Simon Schneider, a Spode teapot arrives on Jo Brayshaw's desk and Cheryl Hakeney's eyes light up when a soup tureen and ladle are served up.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Volcanoes have inspired countless myths and legends, but curiously, they are also major hotspots for UFO sightings; is it possible that these geological formations, which can generate great energy, are attracting otherworldly visitors?

programme.title: UFO: POV
programme.desc: The Federal Aviation Administration refuses to investigate after 12 United Airlines employees report seeing a grey disc-shaped object hovering above Gate C17 at the Chicago O'Hare International Airport, leading some to think it is a cover-up.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.sub-title: The Life and Death of the Sun
programme.desc: Humanity has worshipped the Sun since the beginning of time, and yet even with our modern technology, we still know so little about it. We look if unlocking the mysteries of the Sun can be the difference between life and death for human civilization.

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: While drilling in the Mesa on Skinwalker Ranch, the team makes a discovery that could literally be out of this world.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: For decades UFO research was confined to searching the skies. Now, newly declassified documents suggest mysterious submerged objects are the next field of study.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The Reptilian alien is a fixture of science-fiction, from HP Lovecraft's tales of Valusians to the Cardassians in Star Trek. Could ancient myths about reptilian creatures provide evidence that they are more than just a pop-culture creation?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: A look at why so many people see a vision of Jesus in seemingly normal things such as a tree stump or an antique cabinet.

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Featuring some of the greatest and most impactful mysteries of history. Exploring events that defy rational and scientific explanations, such as flight 191, a notorious LA hotel and more.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth finds himself in the cross hairs after his surprise new hire makes a colossal mistake. Ashley is forced to face one of her most insulting customers yet.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: As Bing gets ready to go to Sula's house, he feels sneezy and hot so Flop decides that Bing needs to stay at home and keep his germs to himself. Even though Bing is soon tucked up on the sofa with a hot honey and lemon drink, he's very disappointed that he can't play with Sula. But Sula and Amma bring him a carroty bagel to wish him a speedy recovery.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: When the Tubby Phone rings, Dipsy comes slowly down on the Dup Dup to answer it - and then goes back up again! The Teletubbies all have fun doing the Dipsy Tubby Phone dance and watch a Tummy Tale about a boy travelling into the treetops.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The adventures of a baby monkey and his mum as they face the challenges of daily life together.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea cheats Chilli out of winning floofy teddies at a funfair made from sweets, so Supertato and the Veggies must get the teddies back before it is too late.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: It's Bing's third birthday! Bing shows Sula, Pando and Coco how to do the waka-oke. When Pando doesn't do it properly, a frustrated Bing hides under his blankey. Flop gently helps Bing to understand that Pando likes to do things differently to him.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo needs to deliver a pizza to a very hard-to-reach island, but some stubborn seabirds have their eye on the pizza. Will Mojo have to go supersonic to get past them?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee looks all around - can he find the Squirrels? Let's play hide-and-seek!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee thought his tie was a little bit plain, so he added some accessories and now it looks amazing. The Squirrels admire Duggee's new look and want to learn how to accessorise themselves. The first step for the Squirrels is to find something they'd like to change. The Squirrels each bring a different item: wellies, a T-shirt, headband, helmet and a potato. They grab sequins, patches, feathers and lights, and then showcase what they have all done.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Tired of being told what to do, Bluey and Bingo enlist their giant invisible friend to show Mum and Dad who's boss. Now they can do anything they want, until Mum and Dad rebel.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo play a game of hairdressers, catering to Mum and all her needs. Then Dad comes along for a haircut, too, and Bluey and Bingo are surprised to discover that he has lice.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grandmaster Glitch builds his effigy in ice, his digging threatens the Lambert Glacier in Antarctica.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of Mr Tod's Trap
programme.desc: When Peter, Benjamin and Lily are tempted by a mysterious hoard of radishes, Lily finds herself caught in Mr Tod's trap. Can Peter find a way to rescue Lily before Mr Tod reaches her?

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Kwazii finds a mysterious, unbreakable coconut. The Octonauts seek out the help of some large, island-dwelling coconut crabs to open it.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Learn to recognise amounts from the shapes they make with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The Alphablocks meet a metal box that goes beep, and soon it's walking and talking and wishing it could be like its new friends.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Nick's looking after Remie and Alva's guinea pigs Celia and Amelia, who are proving quite a handful. Nick fondly remembers his old guinea pig Cyril, and together with his grandchildren, he writes today's song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: A nice and fun song that is all about the joys and magic of reading.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift enjoy making funny honk noises with tubes. They visit Tincy the Spider, who uses terrific tubes to make music with the Playtimers.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula find a helium balloon stuck in a tree in the park. They both want to play with it at the same time so Flop makes two strings and they invent a noisy balloon game called stuck duckies. When they run in opposite directions the balloon flies away, and Flop volunteers to be the stuck duckie so the game doesn't have to end.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'pumpkin', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. There is an orange pumpkin, a bumpy pumpkin and a pumpkin with lots of seeds. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee's friend finally succeeds in saying today's word.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Dermot is upset when he misses a goal and struggles to express himself.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Lou wakes up to find himself on a lily pad in the middle of the lake. He freaks out at first, but his Calm Inner Voice comes to the rescue.

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: After finding some pasta, the Froglets create a rock band with maracas, a drum and a saxophone.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos see a tablecloth covering a table and decide to cover some things of their own. Back in the Boat everyone gets covered in spots! How will they get rid of them?

programme.title: Something Special
programme.sub-title: We're All Friends: How's the Weather?
programme.desc: Justin and friends enjoy ice cream weather, while Mr Tumble dodges a rain shower.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po enjoy a game of musical statues. It takes the Teletubbies a few turns to work out that when the music stops they must stand very still.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Layla, George, Isobel, Lincoln, Jason, Willow and their grown-ups. Inside everyone's bags are different types of paper, which are explored through discovery, play, song and story.

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children explore the forest and sign and sing `Five Little Monkeys'.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Spring is the time of year when lots of different plants grow. Abbie and James join Aaron in the garden for a fun game of big plant, small plant, taking it in turns to spot plants of different sizes. They soon find a tiny one that's just poked its head above the soil - but where has it come from?

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: It's the day of the annual Teddy Bear Party. Murphi is making sandwiches, and Rebecca has made jelly. A little mishap inspires Murphi's first monster make - a tiny toadstool pot. The Mini Monsters create paper cup critter puppets, and Rebecca's cousin, Fiona, calls to share her idea for a cupcake case flower. Murphi's mega monster make is a teddy bear box, helped by some round boxes brought along by the Recycling Express.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: The family puts down their screens to spend time with Bingo and her kindy's Bob Bilby puppet over the weekend.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are out walking around in the rain with Duggee and notice a rainbow. But before they know it, it's gone and the Squirrels are devastated. They decide to make their own rainbow and go off in search of all the colours from everything around them, including strawberries, carrots and bananas. Duggee shows the Squirrels what he has been working on too, a rainbow cake.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee runs the Squirrel Club, where children can earn badges for learning new skills.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Eden and Ava make their very own necklaces using colourful beads. Eden kindly makes one for her best friend and gives it to her as a surprise. Later, the sisters go roller-skating in the park. It's Ava's first time skating on her own, but luckily Eden has lots of good tips for her. Will Ava be able to keep up with her big sister and become a super skater?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Mateo's mum is from Chile and she has shown him how to make a special Chilean dish. Sopaipillas are made with sweet potato dough and served with a colourful pebre made from tomatoes, spring onions and coriander. Mateo decorates his table with lots of plants and colourful flowers. There are even a few alpacas and llamas joining the party! Mateo also shows off his dancing skills to his friends while wearing a traditional Chilean costume.

programme.title: Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is visiting a supermarket, where she finds out what barcodes are and how they work. At the checkout, we learn how an infrared scanner reads barcodes to find out what items we are buying and how much they cost. Maddie uses a special camera to see how the right items in the stock room get to the right shelves in the supermarket. Next, Maddie heads to a factory to see how fishcakes are made.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Diesel is a hearing dog for his teenage owner, Rayane, who is deaf, and they're on a camping trip together. After a breakfast of boiled eggs and toast, Diesel helps Rayane brush his teeth.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie:Tapir and Fruit Bat
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet two tapirs, Inca and Mani - a mother and her son. These incredible animals are almost like three animals in one, with a body resembling a pig, the feet of a rhino and the nose of an elephant. Next, they head into the dark to encounter another animal that loves fruit: the fruit bat, or flying fox. Hanging upside down, the flying foxes feed nearby, giving Aneeshwar and Lizzie the chance to meet these furry-faced bats.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells are on holiday with Gramps, so Waffle is staying at Angela's dog kennels for a couple of nights. During a face-to-face call with Waffle, the Brooklyn-Bells check their wonder dog is settling in and ensure he has his favourite snuggly toy with him, PegStar. Angela tries to settle Waffle but he whines, he's missing his Brooklyn-Bell family and looks at their photo on the wall of his kennel.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: The world outside the burrow has turned white, and Oona and Baba set off with May and Mossy for their first adventure in the snow.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: In this swinging homage to the Sinatra era, the Small Potatoes sing about how they're no average spuds.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Yukee and TapTap spend the night in a tent and are surprised to meet some beautiful, musical fireflies!

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie, an adventurous youngster who happens to be visually impaired, sets out with her best doggy friend Triggs to discover the music and adventure in the sounds of the world around them.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie skips into the Clubhouse and greets her friends. As they greet her, they reminisce about when they first met. Arriving with her parents in a long limousine, Norrie, her parents and her 72 siblings flood out of the car. It's just Norrie who is going to stay at the Clubhouse, though, and enjoy some well-needed quiet. She can play alone and hear herself think.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Four is the new block on the block and he can't wait to share how much he loves to be square.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock E urgently needs fruit to make smoothies. A friendly parrot helps him.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: It's showtime. Justin and his friends put on a play, whilst Mr Tumble has a go at making a puppet theatre, but managing all his actors proves harder than he thought.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po are trying very hard not to make any noise. Then they make as much noise as possible during the Tubby Phone Dance.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how sunflowers play an important role in the environment. They find out that they are made up of lots of tiny nectar-filled flowers, which attract pollinators like bees and butterflies. They also learn that they can be homes for insects like ladybirds, who protect them from other insects that like to munch on sunflowers. On the way, they enjoy some sunflower seed snacks and pretend to be sunflowers following the sun!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Will Jaz get all her tasks done in time to be able to perform her egg dance with her Dad?

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that help with farming. Grace drives a massive compost turner, shows off a machine that makes hay bales and then picks some tasty carrots in a carrot harvester.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: When Biscuit gets peas stuck in her fur, Dodge mishears and thinks she has fleas.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.sub-title: Our Family Fun: Eden and Ava's Superhero Mission
programme.desc: Eden and Ava pretend to be superheroes and go on a mission to rescue their toy!

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: A cold snap hits the Everglades, and Ranger Marsh calls the Octonauts for help.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Seed Snatch
programme.desc: When Mr McGregor fills a bird table with sunflower seeds, Squirrel Nutkin becomes dangerously obsessed with raiding it.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Roly makes his usual noise in the Clubhouse, Duggee and the Squirrels reminisce about how completely different Roly was when he first arrived. Hiding behind his dad, Roly wasn't loud at all but impossibly quiet. As Duggee shows Roly around the Clubhouse, he squeals, hides and hangs off his dad, and just seems altogether too shy to fit in. Duggee has a great idea to introduce Roly to Norrie, thinking a friend will help him to settle in.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Sunny is writing a book about his favourite vet, but first he needs to ask her questions. Vida lets Sunny tag along on a day of house calls, so she can tell him everything he wants to know.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Chilli decides to become a superhero called Super Chilli. His superhero skills are put to the test when Evil Pea covers Supertato in sticky honey, but Chilli's heat saves the day.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston is in costume as everyone's favourite character, Dewey Dragon. But he isn't ready for how demanding Dewey's fans are - Mojo and Bo need to help him escape the crowd.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Tired of being told what to do, Bluey and Bingo enlist their giant invisible friend to show Mum and Dad who's boss. Now they can do anything they want, until Mum and Dad rebel.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The shadow of a monster causes panic among the Piris, apart from Nugget, who is keen to investigate a new mystery. She finally tracks down the creature, but is all exactly as it seems?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo play a game of hairdressers, catering to Mum and all her needs. Then Dad comes along for a haircut, too, and Bluey and Bingo are surprised to discover that he has lice.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: Dr Ollie forgets who he asked to play the wizard in his play! Ava's memory-jogging machine helps him recall, but things get confusing at the nursery.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: While Zoe helps Doug with his homework project about Alaska, Simon and Jess take Waffle and Evie to the shops to buy new school shoes. But Waffle's attempts to help end up causing chaos.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi multiplies to tackle tasks, but chaos turns to harmony when she finally takes charge.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: It's Time to Make Up a Story
programme.desc: When JoJo can't find her favourite Captain Chloe storybook, Gran Gran suggests they make up their own story. As they create their pirate quest, JoJo learns that when you make up your own story, you get to decide what happens next.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady's jukebox inspires a knitted instrument band.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Nothing is going right for any of the Clangers today. Mother's new plant is drooping, Tiny's new tune doesn't sound very good, Granny's knitting colours clash, Major's rocket has broken down and Small's night light has run out of glow honey. But suddenly a wave of beautiful coloured light from a strange star passes over the planet and has a magical effect, everything changes for the better. All the Clangers are delighted until they notice that the star is fading away before their very eyes.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: With MeMo's help, BaBa learns how to be gentle with animals and delicate objects.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka takes a ride on the Ninky Nonk. The Tombliboos are playing inside their house when the Ninky Nonk stops outside. They decide to join Makka Pakka on the Ninky Nonk.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A different story read by a special guest each night just before bedtime.

programme.desc: Frank offers to paint Ray's house but refuses to paint it the colour that Ray and Debra want.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: As Ray escorts Ally to a father-daughter dance, he realises how grown up and independent his daughter has become, and recalls the day when she was born.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The new station manager, Kate Costas, orders Frasier to make major changes in his show.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Their decision to work together makes Martin think Frasier and Niles both need therapy.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The passing of their least favourite aunt sees Frasier and Niles inspired to help Dad make his dream come true: finishing writing a song for Frank Sinatra.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Kate decides that Bulldog is just fine with all the pranks he's playing on Frasier, despite Dr Crane's obvious discomfort. In fact she encourages Frasier to retaliate, and that's when he gets the idea to use a lizard in a prize draw.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Los Angeles to visit La Frite - a family-owned French restaurant being run into the ground by Andre Ramillion, his son and daughter Celine and Alex, and head chef Martine.

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4's midday news summary.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire, Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole meet Pete, whose former chicken sheds are stacked with unwanted stuff, including a toilet cistern and a dressing room mirror.

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: In Italy, Francesca and Carl race against time to reopen their Italian hamlet for business. Meanwhile, a British-run restored medieval village suffers some damage after a big party.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Rory Bremner in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Samantha and Sam are looking for their dream holiday home in Oliva, Spain, that will work for the whole family. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £85k can buy.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Pastor Simon hopes to boost his congregation with an Easter Sunday sunrise sermon on the beach. Adam and Wayne test their friendship by trying to organise a live podcast.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Jane and Rory's handmade kitchen island won't fit through the gîte door. Lionel and Claire work on their dining room's all-important bespoke fromage cupboard.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, can the Rodriguezes' lucky sale streak continue for Steve and Amanda? And in Murcia, the Browns hunt for a family-friendly holiday home for Steve and Hannah.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Includes sport and weather.

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: In Germany, Paul rides an unusual electric coal-transporting train and a 'car on rails' made from a Trabant, dives underground into a silver mine and drives his biggest steam train yet.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley host the battle of numbers, letters and laughs. Roisin Conaty and Phil Wang play Joe Wilkinson and Lou Sanders. With David O'Doherty.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: It's the Playtime phase as the virgins are encouraged to explore their sexual fantasies and embrace their kinky side - from submission and dominance to bondage and impact play.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The group explore basics and intimacy. They practice kissing. Ellen gets naked. A kink session unlocks Millie's desires. An islander comes out. And another loses their virginity.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is back in Redondo Beach, California, for the conclusion of his challenge to save Zayna Flaming Grill from imploding, and he still faces more questions than answers.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: There's a dessert based on Tower Bridge, a starter that looks like a rugby pitch, trusty lists and over-the-top behaviour as three couples compete in south west London.

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Presenters Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer mix a cookery show with a chat show, where they cook in the kitchen and are accompanied by the week's celebrity guest.

programme.desc: George meets a woman who wants to build a shop and a mobile home out of an old horsebox, but only has £500 to spend. He also meets a couple who are creating a holiday let out of a 1960s milk float.

Her widowed mother recruits a male named Ram Luthra to locate and bring her home; while Jabar hires Shyam to try to find and bring her to him. Both men must now not only confront each other but also the invincible Tatar, who has the police and politicians on his payroll, in a fortified city.

Meanwhile, Monica and Rachel hire an ungainly stripper, Roy - without seeing him first - for Phoebe's bachelorette party. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: It's Phoebe and Mike's big day, but all does not go to plan. programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Sonogram at the End

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica finds it difficult to break up with her latest boyfriend whom everyone likes, Chandler is reunited with his beloved cigarettes, and Phoebe receives a large sum of money.

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the East German Laundry Detergent

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An agent offers Joey a movie role as Al Pacino's naked body double, Ross tries to be less conservative, and Chandler thinks his fantasies have come true when he has an affair with a married woman.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A power cut leaves Chandler stranded in a bank with a gorgeous woman, Ross tries to ask Rachel out on a date, and Joey learns that Monica used to be infatuated with him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Nana Dies Twice
programme.desc: Monica and Ross mourn the death of their grandmother with a little help from the gang, and Monica and her mum come to an unspoken understanding about the relationship between mothers and daughters. Meanwhile, Chandler is irritated by office speculation that he is gay.

programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel's Sister Baby-Sits
programme.desc: Rachel regrets trusting her self-indulgent little sister Amy with Emma, Monica regrets asking Joey to write a recommendation letter to the child adoption agency for her, and Phoebe accidentally thwarts the anniversary surprise Mike has in store for her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross applies for a palaeontology grant, but discovers that Dr Hobart - who allocates such funding - used to go out with his girlfriend Charlie. Meanwhile, Mike wants Phoebe to get rid of one of her bizarre paintings, but neither Rachel nor Monica is keen to take it, and Chandler lies to Joey about watching his audition tape.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Home Study
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler fuss over their apartment as they expect a visit from the adoption worker. Meanwhile, Phoebe and Mike opt for a small wedding and donate the extra money to a children's charity.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Late Thanksgiving
programme.desc: Monica reluctantly agrees to cook Thanksgiving dinner, but locks everyone out when they turn up late as Joey and Ross went to a hockey game while Rachel and Phoebe took Emma to a baby beauty contest. Monica also receives some adoption news.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Birth Mother
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler are forced to pretend to be a reverend and a doctor when their prospective child's birth mother gets the wrong end of the stick. Meanwhile, Joey's date with Phoebe's friend doesn't go well, and Ross is left with an inappropriate outfit for a date with a sexy salesgirl.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Gets Caught
programme.desc: Phoebe and Rachel see Chandler getting into a car with a mystery woman and follow them to a large house in the suburbs. They tell Monica of their fears and are shocked by the truth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where the Stripper Cries
programme.desc: Joey appears as a celebrity contestant on `The $100,000 Pyramid' with its actual host Donny Osmond, but Joey's dubious IQ costs his partner the prize money. Meanwhile, Monica and Rachel hire an ungainly stripper, Roy - without seeing him first - for Phoebe's bachelorette party. Also, Chandler and Ross attend a college reunion and meet an attractive woman they both promised not to date during school.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: It's Phoebe and Mike's big day, but all does not go to plan. Wedding planner Monica is driving the bride to distraction, New York is hit by its worst blizzard for 20 years, and Joey is taking his role standing in for Phoebe's incarcerated father far too seriously. Chandler and Ross are upset that neither of them has a role, until Mike needs to replace one of his groomsmen.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Speaks French
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler discover that their prospective adoptive child's biological father may be a `shovel killer', Ross and Rachel get dangerously close when they go to visit her sick father, and Phoebe struggles to teach Joey French for an audition.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel makes the mistake of going for a job interview at a restaurant where her current boss is also dining. Phoebe discovers she can change her name to whatever she likes - Princess Consuela Bananahammock seems the logical choice, until Mike proves that two can play her game - and Joey, upset that Chandler and Monica are moving, is comforted by an eight-year-old.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Rachel makes a startling announcement, Ross secretly schemes to get her former job back for her. Meanwhile, Joey is so flustered by all of the upheaval in his friends' lives that Phoebe prevents him from learning about the sudden death of his agent. Monica and Chandler make a stunning discovery after they check out the house for sale next door to their new home.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Going Away Party
programme.desc: As Rachel prepares to leave for Paris, the gang throw a farewell party for her. Instead of facing them all together, she takes each of them aside for a private goodbye. One friend is offended when he is excluded. Meanwhile, Monica barks out orders as she packs precisely for her life in the suburbs with Chandler, and they watch closely the pregnant mother of their future adopted child.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The six friends prepare to say goodbye to each other.

programme.desc: A talented American artist's romantic Mexican vacation comes to a tragic end when she's the victim of a brutal murder. The hunt is on for a killer.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn
programme.desc: After a young woman is viciously murdered, police launch a two-part investigation; one to find her killer and a second to identify his victim, Precious Jane Doe.

programme.title: The First 48
programme.desc: A Somali girl is executed in Minneapolis and a young man is left dead in Tulsa when a house party spirals out of control.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Finding Justice for Sade Robinson: Follow a haunting search as Sade Robinson vanishes in Milwaukee and a trail of shocking evidence leaves her family fighting for justice.

programme.title: Predator Hunters
programme.desc: When a man targets a 13-year-old on a chat app, Det. Tony Godwin races to snare him before it is too late. Then, a tip-off exposes a predator's huge collection of child sexual abuse material. An online ad sparks a bold undercover sting.

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: Detectives hope a woman can identify her attacker as she fights for her life after being shot in the face, and investigators race to get a confession after a man is gunned down in a motel car park.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: In a story that gripped the nation, three Cleveland women are rescued ten years after vanishing from the city's streets.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: Beloved teacher Barbara Lewis is poisoned in her classroom, as doctors fight to save her life, Lt. Joe Kenda and his team delve into her personal affairs looking for clues, but the biggest revelation comes from the poison itself.

programme.title: Imperfect Murder
programme.desc: Investigate the carefully planned schemes of murderers and potential killers which fell apart, ultimately leading to justice being served.

programme.title: Imperfect Murder

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy

programme.title: The First 48
