programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Six government agents become trapped inside a high-security safe house after a terrorist attack in downtown Los Angeles. They soon realise they must survive not just the enemy outside, but the potential traitor among them.

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla that's been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: The Meg
programme.desc: A massive creature attacks a deep-sea submersible, leaving it disabled and trapping the crew at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean. With time running out, rescue diver Jonas Taylor must save the crew and the ocean itself from an unimaginable threat: a 75-foot-long prehistoric shark known as the megalodon.

programme.title: Mr. Nice Guy
programme.desc: Martial arts romp about a TV chef who becomes embroiled in a drug lord's search for an incriminating video. The kung-fu fighting cook steps in to save an investigative journalist on the run from a gang of murderous drug dealers, resulting in a potentially fatal mix-up involving her incriminating tape of the gangsters and a children's video.

programme.title: The Accountant
programme.desc: A mild mannered, socially weird government accountant moonlights as a highly trained, and deadly assassin.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning
programme.desc: Ethan Hunt and the IMF team race against time to find the Entity, a rogue artificial intelligence that can destroy mankind.

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire

programme.title: Olympus Has Fallen

programme.title: The Amazing Spider-Man

These skills are put to the test when they come across a genuine magical curse in a haunted forest.

programme.title: So I Married an Axe Murderer

They fall in love and marry, but a series of strange occurrences lead him to suspect that his new wife may be harbouring a dark and deadly secret.

programme.desc: Lily Bloom falls for a charming neurosurgeon after she moves to Boston to chase her dream of opening her own business. However, when her first love suddenly reenters her life, her relationship gets upended, leaving her with an impossible choice.

programme.desc: Dracula's spell turns a woman and her loved ones into a vampire, a mummy, a werewolf and Frankenstein's monster.

Once there, daughter Eliza - after meeting a shaman - can talk to the animals, a skill that stands her in good stead to foil a dastardly plot by poachers to kill a herd of elephants for their ivory.

programme.title: Extinct
programme.desc: Op and Ed accidentally time-travel from the Galapagos Islands in 1835 to present-day Shanghai. They make their way through the city in confusion and make the horrifying discovery that the species to which they belong became extinct shortly after.

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon
programme.desc: On the isle of Berk, a Viking boy named Hiccup defies tradition by befriending a dragon named Toothless. However, when an ancient threat emerges that endangers both species, Hiccup's friendship with Toothless becomes the key to forging a new future.

programme.title: Shrek 2
programme.desc: Ogre Shrek and his new wife Fiona are surprised to discover a letter from her parents, inviting them over for dinner. Fiona is eager to show off her new husband, but her parents don't yet know she's an ogre. Taking Donkey along, they set off for Far Far Away to visit them, but the trip is hampered by the jealousy of a wicked fairy godmother and her hired assassin, Puss in Boots.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesn't last for long as word arrives that the ogre's froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.desc: Based on the comic books.

programme.title: Smurfs

programme.title: Monster Trucks
programme.desc: A high school senior befriends a strange subterranean creature after building a monster truck from bits and pieces of scrapped cars.

programme.title: Peter Pan
programme.desc: In stifling Edwardian London, Wendy Darling mesmerises her brothers every night with bedtime tales of swordplay, swashbuckling and the fearsome Captain Hook. However, the children become the heroes of an even greater story, when Peter Pan flies into their nursery one night and leads them over moonlit rooftops through a galaxy of stars and to the lush jungles of Neverland.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Following along as Bec patches up a bitten koala with the new vets, Ruby and Kerryn, soothing a pack of itchy possums. Then, following along as rescue officer Dani frees a pair of trapped kookaburras.

programme.title: Surviving the Amazon
programme.desc: Each year, more than 200,000 monkeys in Peru are trafficked illegally, and almost all are babies. Follow these little ones as they learn to survive alone.

programme.title: Secrets of the Enchanted Forests
programme.desc: Travelling to the Arctic for a peek at the brutal challenges of life in the boreal forest, then admiring some stellar animal adaptations. Witnessing a secret reindeer superpower, navigating a rodent labyrinth of snow, and learning about moose.

programme.title: Reef Wrecks
programme.desc: Mexico's Yucatan Peninsula houses ships converted to artificial reefs.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Horizons
programme.desc: A rocky landscape, carved over eons by wind, water and fire, these are the Cederberg Mountains of South Africa. These imposing peaks are home to incredible diversity.

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Amanda puts a radio collar on one of the first cheetah cubs born in Malawi in twenty years, the team struggles to give a 4-meter python a physical, and a group of tourists deliver a rambunctious baby monkey they rescued from the pet trade.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Following along as Bec patches up a bitten koala with the new vets, Ruby and Kerryn, soothing a pack of itchy possums. Then, following along as rescue officer Dani frees a pair of trapped kookaburras.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Medium Barrie John joins the `Most Haunted' team at Oxford Castle, which was originally built for William the Conquerer, and was later used as a prison. Will he detect any evidence of ghostly activity?

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The `Most Haunted' team, joined by guest medium Brian Shepherd, visit the Chislehurst Caves and spend two nights investigating purported ghostly phenomena throughout this 'underground city'.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The team visit Kiplin Hall in Richmond, which is the said to be the site of many paranormal phenomena including - Crying heard in a drawing room, a woman in Victorian clothing seen on a staircase and a Second World War airman spotted in a kitchen.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Standing at the Anglo-Scots border, Carlisle Castle has a long and brutal history of battles, takeovers and hauntings. A soldier reportedly died of fright there after he saw the ghost of a woman who was once buried inside the castle walls. What will the team find?

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: The Japanese, at war on the Chinese mainland since 1931, hope for quick victories over the British and the Dutch, but 7 December 1941 brings the infamous attack on Pearl Harbor, and the start of an all-conquering sweep through Southeast Asia culminating in the taking of Singapore.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: America entered conflict after the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, but the conflict in Europe and the conflict in the Pacific were two separate wars. Many Americans would have been content to forget about Hitler and Europe. Despite opposition from congress President Roosevelt, however, was committed to fighting Hitler. Eventually Hitler declared war on America, thus relieving Roosevelt of a difficult decision.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewson family continue their pursuit of classic cars. In the home counties, Derek picks up two expertly crafted replicas of the batmobile. Meanwhile, Dave struggles to contain his enthusiasm for a Second World War Ford jeep.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul picks up a stunning 1961 Vauxhall Cresta, Dave drools over a Ford Sierra Sapphire Cosworth, and Derek refuses a test drive in the world's smallest production car, the 3.5 horsepower Peel P50.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek swaps the chaos of the office for rain-soaked Ripon to collect a 1965 Ford Anglia with just 33,000 miles on the clock. Meanwhile, Dave enjoys the clean lines of a rare Renault Caravalle.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel faces one of his greatest ever practical and ethical challenges as he seeks to provide Hermes, the one-legged tortoise, with three new prosthetic feet. Also, a Rottweiler called Dave urgently needs an upgrade to his previous hip replacements.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Keira, a dog on the brink of starvation, and gentle old Jack Russell Paige, whose owner had to make the heartbreaking decision to give her up after 15 years.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete helps rehabilitate a group of ex-racing greyhounds who have never experienced life beyond the racetrack.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Staffy Boxer Cross Crumble, who's been at the centre in Basildon for over three years, stuck without a foster or permanent home in sight.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike, a cash-strapped young couple struggling to buy their first home, think their prayers are answered when a distant relative bequeaths them a sprawling estate. However, the old house is haunted by the ghosts of its former inhabitants.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Still recovering from her near-fatal accident, Alison finds herself hounded by the ghostly gaggle, who now realise that she can both see and hear them. Meanwhile, Mike's attempts to turn the old house into a home leads to a DIY disaster.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: On Pat's `death day', he prepares himself for his family's annual pilgrimage to the site of his demise. Realising that Alison's gift makes her a conduit between the dead and the living, he begs her to pass on a message from the other side.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: The Smith family embark upon a mammoth declutter. Stacey and the team challenge them to let go of half of their possessions, so that they can beautifully reorganise their life and home. Two years ago, Raaj, her husband Roydel and their toddler son, Ajay, moved in with their two nieces, Simran and Harjoth, and nephew Gurkeerat when their mum (Raaj's sister) passed away after contracting Covid.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team set out to renovate the homes of deserving families, changing their lives forever, but with only nine days to transform each home, and dependent on the generosity of volunteers, the pressure is truly on.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: The historic Royal Albert Hall is in need of urgent repairs, however it is a difficult job. A specialist team is required to restore the building to the highest order before its grand reopening.

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers
programme.desc: Salt water and iron don't mix and Southend Pier, built back in 1888, has seen better days. Now a team of specialists are working hard to prevent it falling into the sea.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Meanwhile, Paul and Dave find a Ford Escort that's been untouched for 30 years and set to work on it.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Hear how the arrival of American forces and an Allied counteroffensive in 1918 helped defeat Germany. Tragically, 11,000 men were killed or injured on Armistice Day.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts return to Wells in Somerset, where they welcome visitors with their valuables. Objects under scrutiny include a valuable bracelet once gifted by Queen Victoria, a small seal used by campaigners for the abolition of slavery, and there is a revealing moment for the owners of a rare tapestry.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts are in Dundee, valuing objects brought along by members of the public. Among the treasures unearthed are a valuable miniature clock smuggled out of Germany in WW II, and the world's first copying machine, invented by a Scot in 1780. Plus, an extraordinary letter sent to a conscientious objector comes to light.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: New. An impressive collection of five iconic cars arrives at Thornton le Dale, including a 1967 Crayford Corsair and a 1966 Ford Zodiac which emerge from a garage in Selby.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Nothing screams 1970s more than a Mk3 Ford Cortina 2000E. Derek Mathewson heads to Brigg to value a 1974 example with classic Coke bottle styling and a sweet Pinto engine.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Hear how the arrival of American forces and an Allied counteroffensive in 1918 helped defeat Germany. Tragically, 11,000 men were killed or injured on Armistice Day.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: Today, more than two-thirds of mammal species are nocturnal, and in this episode we will uncover the intriguing strategies they have evolved, from remarkable senses to teamwork, intelligence and adaptability, which have enabled them to overcome a time we find so challenging. Nocturnal mammals have developed an array of sharp senses. And, unlike us, many are blessed with incredible night vision.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: For millions of years, mammals have evolved to master every environment on Earth, but the planet is changing rapidly, all thanks to one mammal in particular - humans. As mankind reshapes the face of the world, mammals must change with it if they are to survive, let alone thrive. Today, some are finding clever and surprising ways to make the most of this new wild, while others need a helping hand from us.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Mammals

programme.title: Porridge

programme.title: Attenborough's Birds of Paradise
programme.desc: David Attenborough highlights the mystery and scientific revelation surrounding the elusive birds.

programme.title: Attenborough's Wonder of Eggs
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores his passion for birds' eggs. These remarkable structures nurture new life, protecting it from the outside world at the same time as allowing it to breathe. They are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. How is an egg made? Why are they the shape they are?
